# Architecture Boundary Checklist: Split Identity Service

- **Purpose**: Validate that the service split requirements are complete, clear, and ready for implementation planning.
- **Created**: 2026-05-20
- **Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` workflow based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all identity-owned capabilities listed without leaving credentials, roles, claims, lockout, account status, or email verification ambiguous? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, §FR-016, §FR-018, §FR-022]
- [ ] CHK002 Are all user-owned capabilities listed without leaving profile, settings, addresses, or stores ambiguous? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK003 Are cross-boundary account-created and role-assignment messages explicitly required for the two workflows that cross service ownership? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK004 Are admin workflows required to identify whether each action affects identity data, user data, or both? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK005 Are development migration/reset expectations documented for all affected record types? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, §SC-006]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is "same public user ID" clearly defined as a shared reference and not shared data ownership? [Clarity, Spec §FR-020, §FR-021]
- [ ] CHK007 Is asynchronous seller-role propagation clear enough to prevent an implementation that directly writes IdentityService data from UserService? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the requirement clear that lockout/account status belongs only to IdentityService and UserService has no separate profile status? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016, §FR-017, §FR-022, §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK009 Are route-change expectations clear enough to require catalog, gateway, and client updates together? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Do role propagation requirements consistently assign store registration to UserService and seller authorization to IdentityService? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK011 Do email-verification requirements consistently keep IdentityService authoritative while allowing UserService only display-oriented copies? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, §FR-019]
- [ ] CHK012 Do downstream projection requirements align with the shared public user ID decision? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, §FR-020]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 Are sign-in success criteria measurable across buyer, seller, and admin personas? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK014 Are profile, settings, address, and store success criteria measurable without depending on implementation details? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK015 Is the seller-role propagation success threshold explicit enough for planning validation? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK016 Is the development migration/reset completion target measurable and bounded? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are primary scenarios covered for authentication, profile management, seller-role propagation, and development migration? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK018 Are exception scenarios covered for delayed or failed cross-boundary message handling? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK019 Are stale-client or outdated-route scenarios included because route changes are accepted? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK020 Are missing profile and orphaned profile/store scenarios covered for breaking development migration? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK021 Are all assumptions that materially affect planning listed, including breaking migration, route changes, event-driven profile creation, and shared public user ID? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK022 Are planning-level dependencies on ADR, event catalog, API catalog, gateway routes, and clients visible in the requirements? [Dependency, Spec §FR-009, §FR-011, §SC-005]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline during requirements review.
